title: rosa canina var. bihariensis
intro: Rosa canina var. bihariensis, also known as the Indian rose or wild rose, is a variety of the Rosa canina species, which is a type of wild rose native to the Indian subcontinent. The medicinal properties of Rosa canina var. bihariensis include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to support cardiovascular health, reduce symptoms of menopause, and aid in the treatment of digestive issues. The herbal preparations of Rosa canina var. bihariensis include teas, tinctures, and capsules, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
